evidence: This meta-analysis does not employ random forest or impurity-based variable importance methods. Instead, the authors state: "We used a model selection and multimodel inference to examine the relative importance of multiple factors influencing the effects of N enrichment... The relative importance value of each predictor was calculated as the sum of the Akaike weights" and they used "a cut-off value of importance to differentiate important and non-essential predictors." Their methods rely on meta-regression, structural equation modeling, and AIC-based model selection to identify important predictors, not tree-based impurity measures. Therefore, this paper is not subject to impurity-importance bias.
